name: [BUG-XS-004] TC-XS-004 — CORS wildcard * is present even on 401 error responses
description:
**[BUG-XS-004]**

The Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header is returned on ALL responses — including 401 Unauthorized error responses. This means the error body is readable cross-origin by any website.

**What this enables:**
A malicious page can make a cross-origin request with no token, read the 401 body (`"Missing Authorization token"`), and use the distinct error messages (BUG-XS-002) to enumerate token validity from the user's browser — all without any credentials.

**Expected:** CORS headers should only be present on successful responses to allowed origins.
Error responses should not have CORS headers, or should restrict them to the trusted origin.
descriptionHtml:
[BUG-XS-004]

The Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header is returned on ALL responses — including 401 Unauthorized error responses. This means the error body is readable cross-origin by any website.

What this enables:
A malicious page can make a cross-origin request with no token, read the 401 body ("Missing Authorization token"), and use the distinct error messages (BUG-XS-002) to enumerate token validity from the user's browser — all without any credentials.

Expected: CORS headers should only be present on successful responses to allowed origins.
Error responses should not have CORS headers, or should restrict them to the trusted origin.

**[BUG-XS-004]**

The Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header is returned on ALL responses — including 401 Unauthorized error responses. This means the error body is readable cross-origin by any website.

**What this enables:**
A malicious page can make a cross-origin request with no token, read the 401 body (`"Missing Authorization token"`), and use the distinct error messages (BUG-XS-002) to enumerate token validity from the user's browser — all without any credentials.

**Expected:** CORS headers should only be present on successful responses to allowed origins.
